text:
Typically, if the viewport is showing 1/10th of the document, the thumb takes up 1/10th of the scroll bar; if 1/100th, 1/100th. At that point, the thumb is so small, it’s a bad UI experience — hard to see, hard to click on.

Rather, the reduction should be logarithmic, so that even at 1/1000th, the thumb is smaller, but still usable.

elements.text:
Typically, if the viewport is showing 1/10th of the document, the thumb takes up 1/10th of the scroll bar; if 1/100th, 1/100th. At that point, the thumb is so small, it’s a bad UI experience — hard to see, hard to click on.

Rather, the reduction should be logarithmic, so that even at 1/1000th, the thumb is smaller, but still usable.

text: And by thumb you mean the handle itself that you can drag? I don't know the names of things. I've been calling it the scrollbar that lives in the scrollbar gutter. Is it a thumb that lives in the scrollbar?

elements.text: And by thumb you mean the handle itself that you can drag? I don't know the names of things. I've been calling it the scrollbar that lives in the scrollbar gutter. Is it a thumb that lives in the scrollbar?
Yes.

text:
After this experiment and talking to <@UC2A2ARPT> I realized a much simpler solution is to grow the hit area of the thumb.

<https://mastodon.social/@spiralganglion/115469389148175886|https://mastodon.social/@spiralganglion/115469389148175886>

<https://git.sr.ht/~akkartik/carousel2.love/commit/c84cda3eec839c25fafddb2eb98b02bcb3244360|https://git.sr.ht/~akkartik/carousel2.love/commit/c84cda3eec839c25fafddb2eb98b02bcb3244360>

text: Yes, this is an even better solution.

elements.text: Yes, this is an even better solution.

text: There might still be some role for logarithmic calculation. One remaining problem is that it's hard to get to the right line in a large file. I'll still thinking about that one.

elements.text: There might still be some role for logarithmic calculation. One remaining problem is that it's hard to get to the right line in a large file. I'll still thinking about that one.

text:
Hi all - here are some of my music projects:

*Minutes*
<https://www.listenfaster.com/main/1min/> is my daily sound journal, where I put up at least a minute of sound a day - a sketch, a demo, a field recording, something I heard that day, a thought and a picture.  Totally for my own reflection but I think anyone would benefit from this kind of practice, regardless of your creative outlet.

elements.text: Hi all - here are some of my music projects:
Minutes

is my daily sound journal, where I put up at least a minute of sound a day - a sketch, a demo, a field recording, something I heard that day, a thought and a picture.  Totally for my own reflection but I think anyone would benefit from this kind of practice, regardless of your creative outlet.

text:
*The Guitar Cult*
A lot of pre-composed guitar ensemble stuff for a band of guitarists I put together. Focused on lots of interlocking things that are fun to play together.  <https://www.theguitarcult.com/visual-evidence/>
I have a publisher who also taught an ensemble course during covid, and the students made videos for these pieces of mine they learned.  They're kinda slick. A very nice thing to happen to me during lockdown.

<https://youtu.be/hmvI6H64SPI>

<https://youtu.be/hmvI6H64SPI>

Our first performance had 11 guitars with Ebows, and I tried to make some Xenakis/Ligeti-like soundmasses with them.

<https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-V9oJ5U5RzQ>

elements.text: The Guitar Cult
A lot of pre-composed guitar ensemble stuff for a band of guitarists I put together. Focused on lots of interlocking things that are fun to play together.

I have a publisher who also taught an ensemble course during covid, and the students made videos for these pieces of mine they learned.  They're kinda slick. A very nice thing to happen to me during lockdown.
Our first performance had 11 guitars with Ebows, and I tried to make some Xenakis/Ligeti-like soundmasses with them.

text:
*Thuja*
Lastly I'm working on documenting and cleaning up my music library Thuja into something 'ready to share' with the FoC crowd. <http://github.com/benmca/thuja>

Here are some of the sounds made with Thuja: <https://www.listenfaster.com/main/tags/thuja/>
